title: NOC 94213 – Industrial painters, coaters and metal finishing process operators | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s industrial sector is prioritizing durability and corrosion resistance as part of its infrastructure and EV battery supply chain goals. Industrial Painters, Coaters, and Metal Finishing Operators (NOC 94213) are the technical experts who apply protective and decorative finishes to a vast array of products—from massive structural steel beams and aircraft components to consumer appliances and automotive parts. As the industry shifts toward "Smart Coating" technologies and automated spray lines, international workers with experience in electrostatic powder coating, chemical dipping, and robotic painting are in high demand across Ontario, Alberta, and Quebec.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Occupations usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group942 – Assemblers and inspectors in manufacturing

duties: Surface Preparation: Cleaning and preparing metal surfaces using sandblasting, chemical washing, or grinding to ensure maximum coating adhesion.
Equipment Operation: Operating automated spray lines, electrostatic coating systems, or dipping tanks to apply paint, enamel, or anti-corrosion chemicals.
Precision Application: Manually painting or touch-up finishing complex parts where automated systems cannot reach.
Quality Inspection: Using digital gauges to check coating thickness, adhesion, and surface finish to ensure compliance with engineering standards.
2026 Update: Utilizing digital tablets to log chemical usage for environmental reporting and monitoring real-time VOC (Volatile Organic Compound) sensors.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Training: On-the-job training is provided; however, a College Certificate in Industrial Painting or a similar technical field is a significant asset.
Experience: Previous experience as a labourer in a manufacturing plant is often required for advancement.
Safety: Mandatory 2026 WHMIS and specialized training in "Respirator Fit Testing" and "Hazardous Material Handling."
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) expects a "Balanced" market. While automation simplifies some high-volume coating, the need for skilled Process Operators to manage these systems and perform quality inspections is projected to grow. Over 4,200 openings are projected due to the modernization of Canada's industrial infrastructure.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is characterized by a "Consistent Demand" for technical finishing specialists:
Ontario (GTA & Windsor): Very High demand. Driven by the automotive retooling for EVs and the massive infrastructure projects in the Greater Golden Horseshoe.
Alberta (Edmonton/Calgary): Strong demand. Essential for the corrosion protection of specialized equipment in the energy, mining, and agricultural sectors.
Quebec: Good prospects. Focused on high-precision aerospace coating and specialized transport manufacturing in the Montreal region.
British Columbia: Stable demand. Driven by the shipbuilding and marine maintenance sectors in Vancouver and Victoria.
National Trend: Approximately 15,800 people work in this occupation. The market favors those who can manage automated spray systems and complex chemical-pretreatment lines.
Why Work in Canada as an Industrial Painter or Coater?
Technological Shift: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including robotic spray booths, high-efficiency powder coating systems, and digital thickness-monitoring sensors.
Diverse Industry Applications: Your skills are vital in aerospace, automotive, construction, and renewable energy sectors.
Path to PR: Frequently prioritized in Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) "In-Demand" lists and is a core qualifier for the 2026 TR to PR transition programs.
Health & Safety Focus: Modern Canadian facilities utilize advanced ventilation and PPE protocols, making it a safer and cleaner industrial trade than in decades past.
pathways: TR to PR Pathway (April 2026): Industrial painters and finishers are classified as "Essential Manufacturing Workers," making them high-priority candidates for this 33,000-person transition quota.
Ontario Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): This NOC is frequently eligible for provincial nomination for those with a valid job offer in Ontario’s industrial corridor.
Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A streamlined pathway for technical manufacturing workers to obtain Permanent Residency with a provincial job offer.
OINP Express Entry Human Capital Priorities: Periodically targets candidates with experience in specialized manufacturing and surface finishing.
